original_hypothesis.title: AgentMigrator Re-migration Overwrites VirtualInfo
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The AgentMigrator allows the owner to reset migration status, enabling re-migration which overwrites the NFT's pointers and orphans existing VeToken stakers.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: If the owner calls reset(id), the founder can call migrateAgent again. This deploys a new Token, VeToken, and DAO, and calls AgentNftV2.migrateVirtual. The NFT now points to the new DAO. The old VeToken stakers are left holding tokens that point to an orphaned DAO, effectively destroying their governance power and connection to the agent.

proof_of_signal.pattern: reset function allows clearing the migratedAgents flag

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Migration is designed as a one-time event, but an admin override allows dangerous duplication.

operational_questions: Under what operational circumstances would reset(id) be called?
Is there a mechanism to migrate old stakers to the new VeToken?
validation_requirements: Verify old stakers cannot interact with the new DAO

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Owner calls reset(id) due to operational error or compromise
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: New infrastructure is deployed and linked to the NFT

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Loss of governance power for all existing stakers
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires manual compensation or complex state fixes

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Multiple DAOs existing for the same virtualId

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
